headline: Claude displaces developers in infrastructure and feature development roles
body: A developer reported their client is replacing them with Claude for DevOps, infrastructure work, and most feature development, signaling accelerating AI adoption in software engineering. For healthcare, this trend directly impacts how clinical software gets built and maintained. Organizations deploying AI-driven development tools will face workforce shifts, requiring new hiring models and skill requirements for teams supporting EHR systems, interoperability platforms, and value-based care infrastructure. Healthcare IT leaders should assess whether Claude-powered development improves time-to-market for critical clinical tools or introduces governance and compliance risks that regulated environments cannot absorb.
